evidence_quotes.quote: video game publishers exercise over customers.
with the removal of the video game The Crew from digital and physical shops, that what we actually buy is not really ours, but remains under their control.
a product purchased with his own money may be removed, not for legal reasons or judicial seizures, but simply because the producer decides that it should no longer be used.

paragraphs.text: I believe that the DFA should also include safeguards against lenient power that video game publishers exercise over customers. Ubisoft has already shown us, with the removal of the video game The Crew from digital and physical shops, that what we actually buy is not really ours, but remains under their control. A user should not fear that a product purchased with his own money may be removed, not for legal reasons or judicial seizures, but simply because the producer decides that it should no longer be used. This would require a clear regulatory framework that prevents publishers from deleting or restricting access to a lawfully purchased product. If this does not change, talking about digital property becomes only unillusionary.
